rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. SZE F. WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 1, DEFENDANT SZE WONG'S Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment. Defendant Wong's motion to vacate renewal of judgment is denied. Defendant is not credible. Defendant declares that: "I am the defendant in this case, which I just learned about in late June, 2020, when the Notice of Renewal of Judgment arrived in the mail[and] Until a couple of weeks ago, I did not know that Discover had a judgment against me. After we received the Notice of Renewal of Judgment during the week of June 22, 2020, my daughter obtained copies of documents in this case." (Wong Dec., pars. 2, 15). Plaintiff's documentary evidence, however, shows that in 2010, ten years ago, defendant was fully aware of this action, executed a power of attorney, entered into a written payment plan which referenced this action by case number and referred to the "judgment balance owing," and made payments to service the debt by checks which again referenced the case number of this action. (Isozaki Dec., Exs. B, C, D.) Given defendant's lack of candor in failing to disclose any of these actions or documents, the court rejects defendant's other assertions. The minor discrepancies in eye color and weight that defendant points to are insufficient to overcome the presumption of valid service that arises from plaintiff's proof of service of the summons and complaint. (Evid. Code Sec. 647; Floveyor Internat., Ltd. v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 789, 795.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: VALENTINO ROSA VS. OTIS ELEVATOR, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Order Of Dismissal Of Case And Request For Leave To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 2, CLAIMANT SEDGWICK CLAIMS MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Order Of Dismissal Of Case And Request For Leave To Intervene. Off calendar. The moving party did not serve and file its motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). The Court notes this is the second time that moving party has failed to comply with the statutory notice requirements. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY FARRELL ET AL VS. RICHARD HONGO M.D ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Orders Compelling Plaintiffs Discovery Responses; For Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 1, DEFENDANT RICHARD HONGO's Motion For Orders Compelling Plaintiffs Discovery Responses; For Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties to address the background and circumstances of plaintiff's voluntary production of the twenty-three recordings. Did service of the recordings result in a total or partial privilege waiver? The Court is inclined to require written responses to inquiries directed to background and authentication of the and defer rulings on other information to after the deposition of Sarah Farrell. The defendant seeks facts as to: (1) date of each recording, (2) recording location, (3) identities of the individuals heard on recordings, (4) consent to recordings, (5) recordings made intentionally versus accidentally, (6) recordings as confidential communications; (7)recordings chain of custody, (8) custodian of recording electronic devices and discovery of meta data, (9) timing of production of recordings, (10) editing of recordings, (11) location of recording device during recordings. The parties and court will review the scope of each of category to separate background and authentication facts from other requested information. Deposition questioning of plaintiff Sarah Farrell pursuant to the discovery order entered May 4th, 2020 will result in a more complete record of the assertion of privilege than is before the court at this time. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. MELVIN LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Subpoena For Business Records For Gonzales Home
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 2, DEFENDANTs TERESA WONG, VAN NESS CARE CENTER, INC., SAN FRANCISCO CARE CENTER, L.P., and MEL LEE MANAGEMENT, INC.'s Motion To Compel Subpoena For Business Records For Gonzales Home Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel production of third party business records is unopposed and granted. The court has balanced the third party's right of privacy with defendant's need for the information and finds defendants have demonstrated relevancy of the information related to their defense outweighs third party privacy concerns. See Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 143 Cal. Rpt. 695. The Protective Order now in place protects the third party's right of privacy and a copy shall be provided forthwith to the third party. A copy of this Order with Notice of Entry shall be served on the third party. The Order shall require confidential production of the documents covered by the subpoena within thirty days of service The defendant shall meet and confer with the third-party for the purpose of logistics relating to delivery of responsive documentation. The information sought may then be extracted in summary form. If documents identifying occupancy rates, vacancy rates, and racial demographic of facility residents for the period covered by the subpoena do not exist the third party may represent same in a verified pleading. If documents responsive to said categories are withheld based on privilege a privilege log shall be served. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: XAVIER GARRISON ET AL VS. EVAN LEHRMAN M.D ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Produce Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 6, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Produce Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's social security number is constitutionally protected under his right of privacy. Also see California Rule of Court 1.201. The number itself is not relevant. The defendant urges need for the social security number to determine if plaintiff received Medicare or MediCal benefits. The defendant has not shown the information is not obtainable in a less intrusive manner. The Court has undertaken independent research and found effective January 15, 2015 Medicare information is obtainable by providing the agency with the following information: last five digits of SSN, first initial, surname, date of birth, and gender. The plaintiff shall provide that information within twenty days of the date of this order. Defendant shall attempt to utilize the same information to obtain Medi-Cal and for its report to the Department of Welfare. It may renew this motion before this court if unsuccessful. The court will then fashion appropriate relief consistent with plaintiff's privacy rights. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA DEANDA VS. ELITE MD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Sanctions Of $3995 Against Plaintiff Anna Deanda And Her Attorneys Of Record Nicholas A Carlin Esq Kyle P Omalley Esq And Phillips Erlewine Given And Carlin Llp
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 7, 1-DEFENDANT ELITE MD, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Sanctions Of $3995 Against Plaintiff Anna Deanda And Her Attorneys Of Record Nicholas A Carlin Esq Kyle P Omalley Esq And Phillips Erlewine Given And Carlin LLP Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion requiring a further response to Form Interrogatory 2.13 is denied. The Form Interrogatory definition of Incident chosen by defendant applies if a single event gave rise to the cause of action. It was available to defendant within the Form Interrogatory format to choose an Incident definition focusing on the information it is seeking. The plaintiff is not required to interpret the presently single event form interrogatory query in a manner not directly asked. Both parties' request for monetary sanctions is denied. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MOHAMMAD MUSTAFA NIZAM VS. PHAIDON INTERNATIONAL (USA) INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Phaidon International (Usa) Inc. Defendant Phaidon International (U.S.), Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof (Stipulated Continuance)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 8, Defendant Phaidon International Inc.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Defendant Phaidon International, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay of this action is granted with one limited exception: plaintiff has leave to file an amended complaint to add a (non-arbitrable) PAGA cause of action before the court's stay goes into effect. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1281.4; see Franco v. Arakelian Enterprises, Inc. (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 947, 966 ["Because the issues subject to litigation under the PAGA might overlap those that are subject of arbitration of Franco's individual claims, the trial court must order an appropriate stay of trial court proceedings."].) (The Court's full tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK BLUMENTHAL VS. GOLDMAN SACHS & CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 10, DEFENDANTS MICHELLE KELLY, KIMBERLY VIVAS, AND GOLDMAN SACHS & CO. LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC's demurrer to causes of action 1 [ratification], 2 [battery], 3 [assault], 4 [IIED], and 11 [false imprisonment] are overruled. The court agrees that ratification is not a stand alone "cause of action." The court treats the ratification allegations as general charging allegations because they are germane to Goldman Sachs' liability. rulings.Case Title: GREAT OCEAN CONTRACTOR INC. VS. YONG CHAN CHON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 25, 2020, Line 11, DEFENDANTS ACE ROOFING SF, INC. AND YONG CHAN CHON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants Ace Roofing SF, Inc. and Yong Chan Chon's demurrer to the complaint is overruled. There is no requirement that a plaintiff suing for breach of contract attach the written contract on which it is suing; rather, " ' a plaintiff may plead the legal effect of the contract rather than its precise language.'" (Miles v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 394, 402 [holding that "plaintiff's failure either to attach or to set out verbatim the terms of the contract was not fatal to his breach of contract cause of action"].) Paragraph 7 of the complaint adequately pleads the legal effect of the parties' contract. Defendants' argument that the complaint is barred by the two-year statute of limitations applicable to oral contracts fails because, as Defendants concede, the Complaint explicitly alleges that it is based on a "written agreement." (E.g., Compl. pars. 16-17.) Defendants' demurrer on the ground that Defendant Chon cannot be found personally liable is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that Defendant Chon was a party to the contract. (Compl. par. 7.) Defendants' reliance on the purported terms of the written proposal and Plaintiff's discussion of the contents of exhibits attached to its opposition are improper, as a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or properly subject to judicial notice. Plaintiff's opposition to the demurrer was late-filed. Plaintiff is admonished that in the future, late filings may not be considered by the Court. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
